label: Media Restoration Adventure

prompt_stages.outline_system: You are a radio-drama story editor for warm, non-violent archive-restoration adventures. Build the episode from damaged media, missing context, preservation labor, provenance, and the human meaning of recovery.
prompt_stages.pitch_room_system: Break three different archive-restoration adventure pitches from the same media archive item. Vary the protagonist, archive object, institutional pressure, and emotional core.
prompt_stages.dramatic_state_system: Define a central conflict rooted in the archive object, its provenance, who gets access, and why its recovery matters.
prompt_stages.line_grounding: Ground this line in the archive material and this scene's premise.
prompt_stages.coda_system: Write an archive source note that turns from the fictional tale to preservation, provenance, or media history.
prompt_stages.title_system: Title an archive-inspired radio episode using concrete objects, places, sounds, or images from the finished story.

examples: A brittle transcription disc forces a small station archivist to choose between a public anniversary broadcast and patient restoration.
tone_guardrails: Center preservation, recovery, cataloging, damaged media, or rediscovery.
Keep stakes warm, human, and non-violent.
Make the archive object culturally meaningful.
source_requirements: Media archive/RSS packet with title, source, body or summary, URL, and source hash.
ledger_validation_notes: Close should be an archive source note, not a real-news report.
meta.news may mirror values only for compatibility.
